§ 01Parties
{{seniorLenderName}}
{{seniorLoanAmount}}
{{subordinatedLenderName}}
{{subordinatedNoteAmount}}
{{companyName}}
§ 02Purpose

This Intercreditor Agreement establishes the relative priority and rights of the Senior Lender and the Subordinated Lender in connection with the ESOP acquisition financing for {{companyName}}. The agreement is required by the Senior Lender as a condition of the SBA 7(a) loan commitment.

Next step: The final Intercreditor Agreement will be drafted and reviewed by legal counsel for all parties as part of the closing process.
§ 03Priority of Payments
A.Senior Lender first. All payments on the Senior Loan (principal, interest, fees) take priority over any payments on the Subordinated Note. The Subordinated Lender agrees not to accept any payments while the Senior Loan is in default.
B.Subordination period. The Subordinated Note is fully subordinated to the Senior Loan for a period of {{subordinationPeriod}} from the closing date, or until the Senior Loan reaches a DSCR of {{dscrThreshold}}, whichever occurs first.
C.Interest during subordination. Interest accrues on the Subordinated Note during the subordination period at {{subordinatedNoteRate}}. Accrued interest is not paid until subordination is released.
D.Release conditions. Subordination is released when: (1) the Senior Loan is current with no events of default, (2) the company maintains a DSCR of {{dscrThreshold}} or higher, and (3) the Senior Lender provides written consent.
§ 04Payment Waterfall

Upon any distribution, sale, or liquidation event, proceeds are distributed in this order:

1.Senior Lender — all outstanding principal, interest, fees, and costs
2.Subordinated Lender — all outstanding principal and accrued interest
3.ESOP Trust — repurchase obligation reserve (if applicable)
4.Company — remaining proceeds for operations
§ 05Subordinated Lender Restrictions

During the subordination period, the Subordinated Lender agrees to the following restrictions:

A.No acceleration. The Subordinated Lender may not accelerate the Subordinated Note or demand payment while the Senior Loan is outstanding, except as expressly permitted.
B.No amendment. The Subordinated Note may not be amended to increase the principal, raise the interest rate, or shorten the maturity without Senior Lender consent.
C.No collateral. The Subordinated Note is unsecured. The Subordinated Lender may not take a security interest in any asset of the company.
D.No assignment. The Subordinated Note may not be assigned or transferred without Senior Lender consent.
§ 06Default Provisions
A.Senior Loan default. If the Senior Loan is in default, the Subordinated Lender may not receive any payments or distributions until the default is cured.
B.Subordinated Note default. If the Subordinated Note is in default, the Subordinated Lender may not accelerate or take collection action without Senior Lender consent.
C.Cross-default. A default on the Senior Loan constitutes a default under the Subordinated Note. A default on the Subordinated Note does not constitute a default on the Senior Loan.
